MOTION TO STRIKE Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-11-2025 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances (via Zoom videoconference):
Atty. Teresa Li, Esq. of Law Offices of Teresa Li, PC (tel: 415-423-3377) for the Plaintiff.
Atty. Noah Hallam, Esq. of Sims, Lawrence & Broghammer (tel: 916-797-8881) for Defendants James M. Nadeau and Lisa A. Nadeau.
Atty. Jonathan Schneller, Esq. of O'Melveny & Myers LLP (tel: 213-430-6000) for Defendants Airbnb, Inc. and Airbnb Payments, Inc.

Hearing Re: DEFENDANTS AIRBNB, INC., AND AIRBNB PAYMENTS, INC.'s MOTION TO STRIKE Amended COMPLAINT.

Matter argued and submitted. Upon conclusion, the Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendants' (Airbnb, Inc. and Airbnb Payments, Inc.) motion to strike the Second Amended Complaint ("SAC") is denied.

"To support an award of punitive damages on the basis of conscious disregard of the safety of others, a plaintiff must establish that the defendant was aware of the probable dangerous consequences of his conduct, and that he willfully and deliberately failed to avoid those consequences." (Penner v. Falk (1984) 153 Cal.App.3d 858, 867 [simplified].) The court disagrees with plaintiff Thomas Henry's contention that constructive knowledge of a joint venturer or principal necessarily suffices for punitive damages. But the SAC alleges that Airbnb had actual knowledge of the hazardous condition in the Nadeaus' property. (SAC para. 21 ["Defendants, each of them, knew the dangerous conditions have a a probability or likelihood to produce serious injury or death . . . ."].) The court is required to accept the facts pled in the SAC for purposes of this motion.

Order submitted.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Court reporter: Sheila Pham, CSR# 13293 (tel: 415-274-9977 / email: calendar-LA@veritext.com). (301/CVA)

DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-11-2025 Sustained
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances (via Zoom videoconference):
Atty. Teresa Li, Esq. of Law Offices of Teresa Li, PC (tel: 415-423-3377) for the Plaintiff.
Atty. Noah Hallam, Esq. of Sims, Lawrence & Broghammer (tel: 916-797-8881) for Defendants James M. Nadeau and Lisa A. Nadeau.
Atty. Jonathan Schneller, Esq. of O'Melveny & Myers LLP (tel: 213-430-6000) for Defendants Airbnb, Inc. and Airbnb Payments, Inc.

Hearing Re: DEFENDANTS JAMES NADEAU, AND LISA NADEAU's DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT.

Matter not argued / contested. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

James and Lisa Nadeau's demurrer to the fourth cause of action [violation of UCL] in the Second Amended Complaint ("SAC") is sustained without leave to amend. The SAC alleges that plaintiff Thomas Henry's wife booked the property. (SAC, pars. 22-23.) There are no allegations that Henry had any financial relationship with the Nadeaus or the Airbnb entities.

To have standing to pursue a UCL claim, the plaintiff must have "suffered injury in fact and has lost money or property as a result of the unfair competition." (Bus. & Prof. Code, section 17204; see also Kwikset Corp. v. Superior Court (2011) 51 Cal.4th 310, 322.) "While the scope of conduct covered by the UCL is broad, its remedies are limited. A UCL action is equitable in nature; damages cannot be recovered. Civil penalties may be assessed in public unfair competition actions, but the law contains no criminal provisions. We have stated that under the UCL, '[p]revailing plaintiffs are generally limited to injunctive relief and restitution.'" (Korea Supply Co. v. Lockheed Martin Corp. (2003) 29 Cal.4th 1134, 1144 [simplified].) Here, Henry pleads no facts showing that he parted with money based upon defendants' business practice. Rather, Henry refers to his medical bills. Such bills are damages and not a basis for restitution. Henry therefore lacks standing to pursue the UCL claim.

Defendants correctly note that "punitive damages" is not a cause of action, but the court construes the allegations as a prayer for relief that the motion to strike addresses. (McLaughlin v. Nat'l Union Fire Ins. Co. (1994) 23 Cal.App.4th 1132, 1163 ["[i]n California there is no separate cause of action for punitive damages."].)

Order submitted.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Court reporter: Sheila Pham, CSR# 13293 (tel: 415-274-9977 / email: calendar-LA@veritext.com). (301/CVA)

Notice Of Motion To Compel Further Responses Re Defendants James M. Nadeau And Lisa A Nadeaus Responses To Form Interrogatories Set One And Request For Production Of Documents Set One And Seek Sanctionss
Off Calendar May-27-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Hearing Re: PLAINTIFF THOMAS HENRY's Motion To Compel Further Responses Re Defendants James M. Nadeau And Lisa A Nadeaus Responses To Form Interrogatories, Set One And Request For Production Of Documents, Set One And Seek Sanctions.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Plaintiff Thomas Henry's motion to compel the Nadeaus to provide further responses to set one form interrogatories and set one document requests and for monetary sanctions is granted in part and denied in part as to the motion to compel and denied as to monetary sanctions. The Nadeaus' request for monetary sanctions is also denied.

Form interrogatory 4.1. Due to the uncertainty whether the response is complete, the Nadeaus must provide a further response stating that they have provided all requested information as to all policies, including any excess policies.

Form interrogatory 12.7 and Requests 1, 4, 6, 15, . Due to the Nadeaus' making their responses subject to objections and their failure to explain how they understand each of the terms they contend are vague, the Nadeaus must provide further responses that are not subject to any objections and explain their understanding of the terms they contend are vague.

Requests 2, 9, 10, 13, 14, 16, 18, 19, 20, 21, 22, and 26. The requests are overbroad and possibly cover a large amount of information that does not meet the standard of discovery relevance and thus no further responses are required.

Requests 5, 11, 12, 17,and 23. The Nadeaus must provide further responses stating whether they do or do not have any responsive documents in their possession, custody or control and explaining their understanding of each of the terms they contend are vague.

Requests 7, 8 and 27. The Nadeaus have agreed to provide further responses and must do so.

Both sides acted with substantial justification, so both sides' requests for monetary sanctions are denied.

Order signed.

Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (301/HEK)
